text:
A long time ago, on a small island dotting the edge of a vast ocean, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He knew that deep in the heart of winter, when the snowflakes danced in perfect counterpoint to the icy winds, nature found its quiet strength, its strength in resilience. Oliver understood this better than anyone. 

One day, a group of young, adventurous doves, Pip and Quill, landed on his favorite branch. Quill, the braver of the duo, piped up first, "Oliver, we've heard tales of your wisdom. We've seen the snow but haven't experienced its chill."

Oliver chuckled softly, "Tales often overshadow the reality, my little ones. But listen well. The chill is not to scare you, but to remind you of the strength you wear. Every feather in your wings is a testament to your tenacity."
